title: NOC 12111 – Health Information Management Occupations | GTR Immigration
description: Health Information Management professionals play a critical role in Canada’s healthcare system by ensuring the accurate collection, coding, storage, and analysis of patient and clinical data. Classified under NOC 12111, this occupation benefits from strong post-pandemic demand and provides promising immigration pathways toward Permanent Residence (PR) through Canada’s skilled worker programs and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.teer: 2 – Occupations usually requiring a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more years; or supervisory occupations
classification.minor_group: 1211 – Court reporters, transcriptionists, records management technicians, and statistical officers

common_job_titles: Health information management supervisor Health information management technician Health records technician Medical records unit supervisor
duties: Classify and code diseases using standardized classification systems
Collect, code, cross-reference, and store health records
Abstract and analyze clinical and demographic data
Operate information systems to manage and retrieve records
Prepare medical, social, and administrative statistics
Use and maintain specialized software systems
Implement or update health information systems and train staff
employment_requirements: Completion of a recognized two-year college diploma in health record technology or health information management
Eligibility to write the Canadian Health Information Management Association (CHIMA) certification exam, which may be required by employers
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 10,500 new positions for Health Information Management professionals, compared with 10,600 job seekers—making strategic province selection and careful immigration planning essential for overseas applicants.
additional_information: Health Information Management professionals are widely employed in hospitals, clinics, workplace health and safety boards, consulting firms, and healthcare organizations across Canada.

Growing hiring needs in healthcare administration and digital records managementStable long-term employment in clinical and regulatory institutionsEligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Employer-supported immigration opportunitiesAnnual earnings typically ranging from $41,800 to $72,000 CAD
